cross_scene_arcs.title: Protective Duty and Evolving Bond with Sidney
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey consistently acts to protect Sidney through escalating threats, reinforcing a deep trust-based relationship, though his overprotectiveness is met with resistance as Sidney asserts her independence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey rescues Sidney from the press and ensures her immediate safety.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey physically comforts Sidney after she discovers a body, reaffirming his role as her emotional and physical protector.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sidney rejects Dewey's attempts to remove her from danger, creating tension between protection and autonomy.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Professional Marginalization and Reassertion of Competence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey is initially dismissed by authorities despite his experience, but gradually re-enters the investigation through key contributions, asserting his value in identifying threats and analyzing the killer's pattern.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey is ignored when offering insights about the killer's motive.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey actively participates in the investigation, shifting from observer to contributor by identifying potential victims.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His expertise remains underrecognized compared to figures like Gale, despite his relevant experience.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict and Shifting Dynamics with Gale Weathers
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey's relationship with Gale evolves from estrangement and mutual hostility to reluctant collaboration and emotional intimacy, culminating in his fatal attempt to protect her.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey rejects Gale's apology and walks away, formalizing their estrangement.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey dies trying to warn Gale of imminent danger, his final act one of protection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Gale survives but is left unaware of Dewey's sacrifice until it's too late, leaving their emotional reconciliation incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Physical Limitation and Determination in Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Despite a visible limp and past spinal injury, Dewey persists in active duty and pursuit of the killer, demonstrating resolve even as his physical limitations hinder his effectiveness.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey's limp is first revealed as he insists on protecting Sidney.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He continues pursuit despite injury and is killed while attempting to reach Gale on an upper level.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His physical condition may have contributed to his inability to evade or survive the attack.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Posthumous Exploitation and Symbolic Presence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After being killed while trying to protect Gale, Dewey's body is staged in a crucifixion-like pose to shock Sidney, transforming his death into a tool of psychological terror.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey dies with his warning unheeded, his presence cut short mid-action.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: His corpse is displayed prominently in the theater as part of the killers' spectacle.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His death is not immediately avenged, and his body is used to further traumatize Sidney rather than being treated with dignity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Defender to Betrayer: Gale's Fractured Relationship with Sidney
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale begins as Sidney's protector against media intrusion, then betrays her by orchestrating a manipulative confrontation, but ultimately shifts back toward concern when she abandons a call to help Sidney.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale physically defends Sidney from reporters and helps her escape
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale abandons contacting authorities upon seeing Sidney in danger and runs after her
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sidney's trust in Gale remains broken despite Gale's final attempt to reach her

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of the Story: Gale's Ethical Descent and Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale aggressively pursues a sensational news angle, exploits Sidney for footage, and dismisses ethical concerns, until Randy's death forces her to confront the human cost of her ambition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale prioritizes a ratings-driven story over victim sympathy and directs Randy to film without consent
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale discovers Randy's murdered body, marking a turning point in her understanding of the danger and consequences
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Gale's ambition led to Randy's death, but she has no chance to reconcile this before being attacked

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rise and Fall of Authority: Gale's Shifting Status in the Investigation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale gains official status as a consultant due to her notoriety, contributes key insights about the killer's pattern, but becomes a direct target, undermining her position of control.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale is formally welcomed by law enforcement and given access to the investigation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale is actively hunted by the killer, losing her position of influence and becoming a vulnerable survivor
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her expertise is validated, but her lack of formal training leaves her unprepared for real danger

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Killer: Gale's Transformation from Observer to Target
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale transitions from analyzing the killer's behavior to being directly threatened, witnessing Randy's death, evading attacks, and ultimately being betrayed and stabbed by the killer.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The killer calls Gale directly, warning her to stay away from the investigation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale is stabbed by Cotton and thrown into the orchestra pit, left for dead
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Gale's survival is uncertain after being stabbed and discarded

cross_scene_arcs.title: Resilience Under Siege
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney consistently asserts control and independence despite escalating threats, using preparedness and defiance to maintain autonomy in the face of harassment and public exposure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: emotionally guarded but composed, managing trauma with routine
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: defiant under digital attack, asserting resistance despite fear
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: her preparedness may not be enough against an evolving, tech-savvy threat

cross_scene_arcs.title: Unraveling the Conspiracy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney moves from unawareness to confrontation with layered threats, first facing an anonymous killer, then uncovering a staged conspiracy, and finally surviving a personal vendetta tied to her past.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: unaware of the new murders and their connection to her
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: severely wounded but alive after defeating a vengeful mastermind
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether she will fully recover from her physical and psychological trauma

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Trust and Media Exploitation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney's relationships with Gale, Cotton, and Hallie deteriorate due to manipulation and betrayal, revealing how her trauma is exploited for public spectacle and personal gain.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: wary of media attention but willing to engage on her terms
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: betrayed by multiple figures from her past, including those she trusted or forgave
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to rebuild trust in a world that continues to profit from her suffering

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalation of Physical and Psychological Threats
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney transitions from evading attacks to confronting orchestrated terror, experiencing direct violence, psychological manipulation, and digital stalking that erode her sense of safety.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: fleeing first physical confrontation with Ghostface
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: cornered and destabilized by staged betrayal and real violence
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the killer's ability to manipulate both technology and people undermines all protective measures

cross_scene_arcs.title: Dependence and Protection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney's dynamic with Dewey evolves from professional protection to emotional reliance, especially during moments of shock and vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: receiving Dewey's help with guarded acceptance
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: physically and emotionally dependent on Dewey after traumatic discovery
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: her need for protection conflicts with her desire for independence
